content:
Recursive Storytelling: every world artifact is tagged so it can be read as a chapter in that world’s ongoing chronicle, not just a one-off output. Sessions that touch a world must update its wave and arc status.

Example name:
WORLD-MYTH-SPEC-CORE-0100-v0_2

1.5 STORY Layer

Function: encapsulates narrative episodes, character arcs, and interactive sequences.

Metaformeme: STORY artifacts fix and evolve narrative patterns (for example, descent-as-refactor, return-as-integration). These patterns become templates for AIs when designing new episodes.

Myth Fusion: characters are represented as “mythic agents” with attributes, conflicts, and transformations that mirror real workflows or technical challenges.

Wave Theory: episodes are grouped into waves or “seasons” that crest and resolve around specific themes or deliverables. The same wave structure guiding technical work also guides story arcs.

Recursive Storytelling: STORY artifacts are explicitly linked back to WORLD and VISION, so that your fiction, documentation, and operations are facets of the same meta-narrative.
context: From document: 0.	Preamble and Scope — chunk 5 (chunk 5/31)
